Sub: Annual Report of the Company for the Financial Year 2024-25
Dear Sir(s),
Since 1994
This is to inform that the 16th Annual General Meeting ('AGM') of the Company is scheduled to be
held on Friday, September 26, 2025, at 3:00 p.m. (IST) through Video Conferencing/ Other Audio-
Visual Means ('VC/OAVM') in accordance with the applicable circulars issued by the Ministry of
Corporate Affairs and Securities and Exchange Board of India.
Pursuant to Regulation 34(1) of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Listing Obligations and
Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, ('SEBI Listing Regulations') we are submitting herewith
the Annual Report of the Company along with the Notice of 16th AGM and other Statutory Reports for
the financial year 2024-25, which is being sent only through electronic mode to the Members.
The Annual Report containing the Notice is also uploaded on the Company's website and can be
accessed at www.kpenergy.in.
ENERGY IS HEDE
We would further like to inform that the Company has fixed Friday, September 19, 2025, as the cut-
off date for ascertaining the names of the Members, holding shares either in physical form or in
dematerialised form, who will be entitled to cast their votes electronically in respect of the businesses
to be transacted as per the Notice of the AGM and to attend the AGM.
You are requested to take the same on your records.
Thanking you,
For K.P. Energy Limited
KARMIT
Digitally signed by

Annual Report
2024-25
Sustainable
Growth,
Accelerating
Momentum

Sustainable
Growth,
Accelerating
Momentum
KP Energy's performance this year
represents 'Sustainable Growth,
Accelerating Momentum' - a
statement that echoes through
every achievement, milestone, and
innovation. True to its ambition, KP
Energy has not just sustained its
upward trajectory, but has propelled
it to new heights, integrating
sustainability into the very
foundation of its record-breaking
performance.
This year, KP Energy has delivered exceptional
financial results, outpacing its own benchmarks
and setting new standards for the industry. Each
metric - top line, bottom line, and every Key
Performance Indicator (KPI) in between - reflects
a Company not content with steady progress, but
one with intent on accelerating its momentum.
This momentum is more than numbers; it
speaks to operational excellence, superior project
execution, and a deep-rooted commitment to
powering India's renewable energy ambitions. KP
Energy's contributions to the sector have added
real mileage to the nation's journey toward its 2030
renewable capacity targets, making the Company
a vital force in the country's energy transition.

Y-o-Y growth
As a leading turnkey EPC player
in India's most promising wind
geographies, KP Energy continues
to unleash growth, innovation, and
sustainability, living the motto of
'Sustainable Growth, Accelerating
Momentum' every day. The
Company's unique 3-pronged
business model acts as a diversified
foundation, allowing it to harness
industry tailwinds and seize the
opportunities presented by the
global shift to clean energy.
This year, KP Energy crossed
a landmark, with 1+ GW of
cumulative renewable capacity
energized, a clear reflection of its
accelerating momentum. The road
ahead looks even more promising,
with a robust order pipeline
exceeding 2.26 GW, setting the
stage for sustained growth and
continued leadership.
KP Energy's journey of momentum
doesn't stop at numbers. The
Company is proactively exploring
new avenues like offshore wind
projects, expanding into new
geographies, and building on
its legacy in Gujarat's onshore
segment. Each step forward
is powered by robust project
planning and execution,
technological innovation,
and strategic partnerships, all
underpinned by a commitment
to sustainability.
As we look to the future, KP
Energy remains steadfast
in its mission, driving India's
energy transition with inventive,
environmentally responsible
solutions, nurturing a culture
of excellence, and delivering
superior value to clients and
shareholders. With accelerating
momentum and a foundation of
sustainable growth, KP Energy
is poised to lead, inspire, and
shape the renewable energy
landscape for years to come.
Safe Harbour Statement
In this Annual Report, we have disclosed forward-looking information to enable investors to comprehend
our prospects and make informed investment decisions. This report and other statements - written and
oral - that we periodically make, contain forward-looking statements that set out anticipated results
based on the management's plans and assumptions. We have tried wherever possible to identify
such statements by using words such as 'anticipate', 'estimate', 'expects', 'projects', 'intends", plans',
'believes", and words of similar substance in connection with any discussion of future performance.
We cannot guarantee that these forward-looking statements will be realised, although we believe we
have been prudent in assumptions. The achievement of results is subject to risks, uncertainties and
even inaccurate assumptions. Should known or unknown risks or uncertainties materialise or should
underlying assumptions prove inaccurate, actual results could vary materially from those anticipated,
estimated or projected. Readers should bear this in mind. We undertake no obligation to publicly update
any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

GROUP OVERVIEW
A force behind
India's green
energy revolution
At the forefront of green innovation
The KP Group stands as an eminent force in the renewable energy
landscape, representing excellence in sustainable energy solutions across
India. Since its establishment in 1994 by Dr. Faruk G. Patel,
the organization has transformed from a modest logistics service provider
into a distinguished conglomerate with comprehensive expertise
spanning the entire renewable energy spectrum. With over 3 decades
of entrepreneurial excellence, the Group has strategically diversified
its operations to encompass 50+ companies and organizations, each
contributing to sustainable energy development and social progress.
50+
Companies &
Body Corporates
The KP Group now comprises
50+ entities contributing to its
rapid expansion.
Diverse Renewable
Energy Portfolio
Key areas include solar energy,
wind energy, hybrid energy,
fabrication & galvanizing
products for renewable energy
projects, fabrications for telecom
& infrastructure applications, and
emerging technologies such as
green hydrogen and ammonia
technology, BESS, offshore solar
and wind.
145036
Corporate Vision &
Strategic Commitment
Capitalizing on country's
industrial expansion,
economic vitality, and
vast renewable energy
potential, KP Group has
established itself as a
leader in the renewable
energy sector. With
a strong focus on
environmental stewardship
and innovative, future-
ready practices, the Group
is dedicated to helping
businesses achieve
energy self-sufficiency.
This commitment paves
the way for a sustainable
and prosperous future
- benefiting both
its partners and the
communities it serves.
By harnessing the
power of solar, wind,
and hybrid energy
solutions, KP Group
pushes the boundaries
of conventional energy
practices to meet the
evolving needs of its
customers.
KP Group is deeply
committed to securing
a greener future for
the generations ahead.
Core Business Interests
Solar
Energy
Owner of one of Gujarat's largest private
solar parks, leading development of
captive solar projects for industrial
clients with comprehensive end-to-
end solutions across India, while also
planning to venture into floating solar
to conserve resources and enhance
efficiency.
Wind
Energy
Gujarat's premier Balance of Plant
(BOP) wind energy solution provider,
offering complete project lifecycle
management from conceptualization to
commissioning with advanced technical
expertise, while also exploring offshore
opportunities where consistent high-
speed sea winds near Gujarat and Tamil
Nadu promise high PLF power, port
infrastructure growth, and job creation;
KP Group is actively evaluating and
participating in offshore wind tenders.
Hybrid
Energy
Integration of wind and solar
technologies for enhanced efficiency,
providing higher energy output and
improved cost-effectiveness through
comprehensive project development
capabilities.
Renewable
Infrastructure
Complete one-stop solution for
sustainable renewable energy
infrastructure including solar and wind
mounting structures, transmission line
infrastructure, windmill lattice tower
structures, and substation equipment
structures.
H2
Green
Hydrogen
Development of cutting-edge green
hydrogen production capabilities,
positioning for leadership in India's
emerging sector with focus on clean
energy technology advancement and
industrial decarbonization.
BESS
Solutions
Investment
KP Group is venturing into Battery
Energy Storage Solutions (BESS)
to support grid stability, peak load
management, and round-the-
clock renewable supply.

and sustainability, the Group

of one of Gujarat's largest private
solar parks. At the heart of KP
Group's vision is a steadfast
commitment to a greener future,
leveraging clean energy solutions to
meet the evolving energy demands
of a rapidly transforming India.

GROUP OVERVIEW (CONTD.)
KPI
Green Energy
Limited
Incorporated in 2008, KPI Green
Energy has emerged as one of
Gujarat's - and India's - leading
renewable energy companies.
Recognized as a market leader
in clean energy generation and
third-party power sales within
Gujarat, the company has built a
strong reputation for its expertise,
innovation, and consistent
performance in the renewable
energy sector. As on FY25, KPI
Green Energy operates across
77 site locations in Gujarat and
Maharashtra, highlighting its wide-
reaching impact.
The company proudly operates
one of the largest private-sector
renewable energy plants in Gujarat
and has set an ambitious Group
target of achieving 10+ GW of
renewable energy capacity by
2030 - in alignment with India's
national goal of reaching 450 GW of
renewable capacity by the same year.
Our Business Verticals
. Independent Power
Producer (IPP): We develop,
build, own, and operate
renewable energy plants,
ensuring a continuous, reliable
supply of clean energy for third-
party customers.
. Captive Power
Producer (CPP): We offer
customized renewable energy
solutions for businesses and
industries seeking to reduce
electricity costs and drive their
sustainability goals.
This dual-vertical approach enables
KPI Green Energy to operate
both as a power producer and a
comprehensive service provider
for government, corporate and
industrial clients. Our capabilities
extend beyond solar, including
hybrid energy solutions that
effectively meet diverse and
evolving energy requirements.
We specialize in the design,
development, ownership, operation,
and maintenance of high-
performance renewable power
plants. This full-spectrum expertise
allows us to deliver efficient,
scalable, and future-ready energy
infrastructure.
KPI Green Energy is firmly
committed to leading the transition
to a more sustainable energy future.
By focusing on renewable, reliable,
and cost-effective solutions, we
continue to expand our power
generation capacity and actively
contribute to India's journey toward
a net-zero carbon economy.
Vision
Powering INDIA by the
power of Nature

KP Green
Engineering
Limited
(Formerly known as K P Buildcon Private Limited)
KP Green Engineering Limited,
formerly known as K P Buildcon
Private Limited, is the flagship
company of the renowned KP
Group, founded by Dr. Faruk G.
Patel. Incorporated in 2001, the
company builds on the KP Group's
legacy, which dates back to 1994.
Originally focused on the fabrication
and hot-dip galvanization of steel
products, KP Green Engineering
has since evolved into a diversified
and dynamic enterprise. In 2024,
it marked a major milestone
by transitioning to a publicly
listed company, symbolising its
remarkable journey of growth and
transformation.
Over the past 24 years, KP Green
Engineering has significantly
expanded its presence across India.
Its unwavering commitment to
sustainable and innovative solutions
has driven success across multiple
sectors, including fabrication,
galvanization, renewable energy,
telecom infrastructure, and other
emerging infrastructure arenas.
Following the successful completion
of its SME IPO, the company
commissioned a major greenfield
capacity expansion at its Matar,
Bharuch facility. Alongside with its
Por and Kural units, this strategic
investment has significantly
augmented KP Green Engineering's
product portfolio and production
capabilities. Its state-of-the-art
fabrication facility, outfitted with
advanced CNC machinery, is
designed to manage complex, large-
scale industrial projects. Currently,
the company boasts an installed
production capacity of 142,500 MT
per annum, which is projected to
scale up to 400,500 MT per annum
by FY26, positioning it as home to
Asia's largest hot-dip galvanizing
plant.
KP Green Engineering's
commitment to quality is reflected
in its ISO 9001:2015 certification and
comprehensive vendor approvals
from prestigious organizations
such as GETCO, BSNL, GNFC, EIL,
MAHATRANSCO, among others -
underscoring its reputation as a
trusted industry partner.
Driven by a passion for technological
innovation, operational excellence,
and customer satisfaction, KP
Green Engineering continues to
lead from the front. Its consistent
delivery of high-quality, future-ready
solutions has earned it a reputation
as a preferred supplier and a reliable
partner in India's infrastructure
development journey.
With a strong foundation and
visionary leadership, KP Green
Engineering not only reflects
the success of the KP Group but
also defines its vision for India's
sustainable and industrially
empowered future.
Vision
Powering INDIA by the
power of Nature.

GROUP OVERVIEW (CONTD.)
KPI Green Hydrogen
and Ammonia
Private Limited
Headquartered in Gujarat, the
Company is an emerging player in
the green hydrogen and ammonia
sector, leveraging renewable energy
from group companies through
sources like solar and wind to fuel
eco-friendly innovations across
the industrial and energy sector.
Green hydrogen is generated via
electrolysis - a carbon-free process
using clean electricity to split
water into hydrogen and oxygen,
significantly reducing greenhouse
gas emissions compared to
traditional, fossil-based methods.
Vision
To be a leader in the
transition to a low
carbon world. To support
economic development
in Green Hydrogen in
India and near regions by
providing our clients with
the most suitable and
competitive green energy
solutions.
Founded in
2024
Sector
Green Hydrogen &
Ammonia
Core Offerings and
Projects
. Green Hydrogen & Ammonia
Production: KP Green Hydrogen
and Ammonia will be producing
high-purity green hydrogen
and ammonia. Its state-of-
the-art upcoming plant will
guarantee efficient, sustainable
output, playing a key role in
decarbonizing industries such as
transportation, power generation,
agriculture, and manufacturing.
. Flagship Project: The company
has launched Asia's first 1 MW
green hydrogen plant in Matar,
Bharuch. Operating as a model
for future expansion, this facility
blends green hydrogen with
LPG to fuel large industrial
operations, demonstrating both
environmental impact and
industrial viability.
. Circular Solutions: The company
will also pioneer biomass-
to-hydrogen technology,
transforming agricultural
waste into clean hydrogen -
offering added value through
waste reduction and local
empowerment.
HYDROGEN
ENERGY
STORAGE
1 MW Green

Established in Matar, Bharuch,
Gujarat, blending green

industrial use.
End-to-End
Green Solutions
Offering a full spectrum of
green hydrogen and green
ammonia solutions across bulk
deliveries, onsite generation
(including BOO/BOOT models),
and application development
for diverse industries.
Biomass-to-
Hydrogen Innovation
Plans to utilize a proprietary
process to convert agricultural
and organic waste into green
hydrogen, supporting the
circular economy, reducing
pollution, and empowering
rural communities through
decentralized production.
Fleet
Transition Plan
Actively rolling out
hydrogen-powered vehicles for
employee transport, beginning
the shift from diesel to fuel-cell
mobility and demonstrating
practical hydrogen adoption.
KP Human
Development
Foundation
Established in 2015, the KP
Human Development Foundation
functions as the Corporate Social
Responsibility (CSR) arm of the KP
Group. The foundation is committed
to driving meaningful social impact,
with a primary focus on enhancing
access to quality education for
underprivileged children. In
collaboration with both government
and private institutions, it works
to raise educational standards
and increase opportunities for
marginalized youth, thereby
empowering them to build better
futures.
At its core, the foundation aims to
make education accessible and
equitable. It supports and adopts
schools to ensure that quality
learning reaches those who need
it most. Beyond its educational
initiatives, the foundation is actively
involved in a wide range of social
welfare programs. These include
providing essential support for the
elderly, offering care for orphans,
delivering medical assistance
to underprivileged individuals,
and promoting environmental
sustainability through activities such
as large-scale tree plantation drives.
Rooted in a strong sense of social
responsibility, the KP Human
Development Foundation seeks to
address the broader needs of society
through inclusive, sustainable, and
impactful interventions. Its holistic
approach is designed not only
to respond to immediate social
challenges, but also to foster long-
term community development.
With a vision to uplift educational
standards and improve accessibility,
the foundation continues to make
a significant difference in the lives
of individuals and communities.
Through its diverse initiatives, the KP
Human Development Foundation
ensures that the KP Group's
commitment to social responsibility
is translated into real, lasting benefit
- laying the foundation for a
brighter, more inclusive future for all.
Vision
A world in which every
human attains the right
to Survival, Protection,
Education, Development
and Participation.

COMPANY OVERVIEW
Empowering India's
wind revolution
KP Energy Limited (KPEL) stands as Gujarat's and one of India's foremost
providers of Balance of Plant (BOP) solutions in the wind energy sector. As a
trusted partner for India's green energy transition, KPEL delivers seamless,
comprehensive BOP solutions that span the entire lifecycle of wind and wind-solar
hybrid energy projects - right from initial concept and site identification to project
realization, commissioning, and ongoing operations and maintenance. Renowned
for its innovation, technological prowess, and commitment to turnkey excellence,
KP Energy is setting new benchmarks in the renewable energy industry.
Harnessing the boundless
power of the wind and
solar, we transform vision
into reality - driving India's
clean energy revolution
with every project we
deliver.
Track Record of
Leadership
Since its inception in 2010, KP
Energy has successfully executed
projects of varying scale across
challenging and remote locations,
serving a diverse clientele that
includes leading independent
power producers and leading
renewable majors. The Company
has energized a cumulative
installed capacity of 1+ GW, with
an additional 2.26+ GW in the
pipeline, underscoring its robust
project execution capabilities
and industry leadership. KPEL's
comprehensive suite of services
covers every critical aspect
of wind and hybrid project
development - site identification,
meticulous site preparation,
efficient construction and
erection, and seamless power
evacuation - delivering end-to-
end solutions that ensure quality
and reliability at every stage.
Diversified Business
Segments
KP Energy's business model is
further strengthened by two
ancillary segments:
. Independent Power Producer
(IPP): KPEL has a portfolio of 48.5
MW of operational renewable
energy assets, including 37 MW
of wind and 11.5 MW DC solar
energy assets. These assets
generate annuity revenue
streams, enhance business
resilience, and contribute directly
to India's clean energy goals.
. Operations & Maintenance
(O&M): Through its wholly
owned subsidiary, KP Energy
OMS Limited, the Company
delivers specialized O&M services
for BOP infrastructure, ensuring
uninterrupted operations and
long-term project continuity for
clients.

JOURNEY & MILESTONES
From foundation
to leadership
KP Energy has traversed a transformative journey since its
inception, marked by significant achievements and transitions
that have shaped its evolution into one of the key stakeholders
in India's renewable energy sector. The Company's growth
has unfolded in distinct phases, characterized by strategic
expansion, operational excellence, and continuous innovation.
Over the years, KP Energy has demonstrated resilience and adaptability, overcoming
industry challenges and capitalizing on emerging opportunities. Its portfolio now
includes 1+ GW of energized renewable capacity, with a robust pipeline exceeding
2.26 GW, underscoring its strong execution capabilities and market leadership.
This year represents another milestone for KP Energy, as the Company achieved
record breaking operational and financial performance, surpassing all previous
benchmarks. Building on this momentum, KP Energy is well-positioned to pursue
new growth avenues and further redefine standards within the Indian renewable
energy industry.

BUSINESS VERTICALS
Comprehensive
capabilities
across verticals
KP Energy's robust business model is anchored in three
synergistic verticals: Engineering, Procurement, Construction and
Commissioning (EPCC), Operation and Maintenance (O&M), and
Independent Power Producer (IPP). This strategic diversification
empowers the Company to offer comprehensive solutions across
the entire wind and wind-solar hybrid energy project lifecycle,
building resilience, expanding market penetration, and ensuring
sustained value creation for all stakeholders.
1.
Engineering,
Procurement,
Construction and
Commissioning
(EPCC)
Flagship Division and
Core Growth Engine
The EPCC vertical stands at
the forefront of KP Energy's
operations, serving as the primary
growth contributor and a key
differentiator in the renewable
energy sector. Through this
vertical, KP Energy delivers a
unique and comprehensive
value proposition, addressing the
multifaceted challenges faced
by wind turbine manufacturers
(WTGs), independent power
producers (IPPs), and captive
power producers (CPPs) during
the development of utility-scale
wind and wind-solar hybrid
projects.
Revenue Share by Business
Segments (FY25)
(Consolidated)
96.9%
Comprehensive
BOP Solutions
KP Energy's EPCC services
encompass the entire spectrum
of Balance of Plant (BOP)
solutions, including:
. Site Identification &
Acquisition: Leveraging deep
domain expertise to identify
optimal sites and secure
necessary land and statutory
approvals.
. Detailed Engineering
& Design: Conducting
meticulous site assessments,
geotechnical investigations,
and designing infrastructure
tailored to project needs.
. Procurement & Logistics:
Managing procurement of
materials and equipment,
ensuring timely and cost-
effective delivery to project
sites.
. Construction & Erection:
Executing civil works, electrical
installations, road construction,
and erection of transmission
lines and pooling substations.
. Commissioning & Grid
Integration: Coordinating
with utilities and regulatory
authorities for seamless
commissioning and grid
synchronization.
Turnkey Project Execution
By assuming full responsibility
for project execution, KP Energy
enables its clients to focus on their
core business, while the Company
expertly navigates complexities
related to land, infrastructure,
and statutory compliance. This
turnkey approach ensures timely
delivery, cost efficiency, and high
standards of quality and safety.
Strategic Positioning
With supportive government
policies and a growing demand
for renewable energy, KP Energy's
EPCC vertical is well-positioned
to capitalize on emerging
opportunities. The Company's
proven track record and ability to
deliver large-scale projects across
challenging terrains reinforce its
reputation as a preferred partner
for wind and hybrid energy
solutions.

Extending Value Beyond

Recognizing the critical

Comprehensive O&M Services
KP Energy's O&M offerings include:
. Pooling Substation
Management: Continuous

evacuation and grid stability.

Maintenance: Upkeep of high-

voltage (EHV) lines, minimizing

In the initial years post-

continued engagement and
service excellence. This vertical
not only enhances client trust and
satisfaction but also generates
stable, annuity-based revenue
streams, contributing to the
Company's financial resilience.
Serving Internal and External
Clients
KP Energy manages O&M for both
its own commissioned projects
and select third-party assets,
optimizing resource utilization
and maximizing client value.
The Company's commitment to
operational excellence has made
it a trusted partner for long-
term asset management in the
renewable sector.

Power Producer

Streams and Sustainability

relationships, and maintain a resilient, future-ready organization. This strategic alignment not only drives KP Energy's
growth but also cements its leadership position in India's renewable energy landscape.

SCOPE OF EPCC BUSINESS
Unmatched
expertise in
EPCC services
KP Energy is committed
to delivering an extensive
range of services through
its EPCC (Engineering,
Procurement, Construction,
and Commissioning)
business vertical. Our
holistic approach ensures
that we provide true end-
to-end solutions for wind
and wind-solar hybrid
power project development,
covering every phase from
site identification to final
commissioning. This breadth
of expertise and experience
enables us to manage the
entire project lifecycle,
ensuring seamless execution
and optimal outcomes for
our clients. Recognizing that
each project and client has
unique requirements, we
offer both comprehensive
and tailored solutions,
always with the mission to
foster wind and wind-solar
hybrid energy growth and
achieve excellence in project
delivery.
1.
Site Identification
We leverage advanced technologies and
data-driven methodologies to meticulously
evaluate and select the most promising sites
for wind energy projects. Our expertise in
site identification, especially in Gujarat - the
state with the largest wind potential in India-
gives us a significant competitive advantage.
By utilizing satellite imagery, wind resource
assessments, LIDAR technology, and
detailed techno-commercial feasibility
studies, we ensure that each site meets the
highest standards for long-term project
viability and performance.
2.
Site Preparation and Logistics
Effective site preparation is critical for the
success of any wind energy project. Our
experienced teams have executed projects
across a variety of terrains, including coastal
plains, rocky ridges, marshy plateaus, and
low-lying water-prone areas. We specialize in
overcoming logistical challenges, ensuring
that even the largest wind turbine components
- such as blades and nacelles - are transported
and delivered safely and efficiently, regardless
of site complexity. This capability is a
key enabler for smooth project execution
in remote or challenging environments.
3.
Construction and Erection
KP Energy's construction and erection services
encompass all major activities required for setting
up wind farms. We undertake civil works for wind
turbine generator (WTG) foundations, complete
crane platforms, and manage the transportation
and precise installation of WTG components. The
strategic alignment with group entity having own
cranes helps in faster erection and installation
of projects. Our teams also establish the internal
power transmission infrastructure, including
33kV switchyards (USS) and high-voltage lines,
ensuring effective power transmission and seamless
connectivity to substations for power evacuation.
4.
Power Evacuation, Permits,
and Approvals
We take full responsibility for constructing power
evacuation infrastructure, such as substations
and extra-high voltage (EHV) lines, which are
critical for integrating wind and wind-solar
hybrid power into the grid. Our experienced
regulatory team navigates the complex
landscape of permits and approvals, proactively
liaising with relevant authorities to ensure
compliance and expedite project timelines. Our
expertise in securing all necessary regulatory
clearances is a key factor in our ability to deliver
projects on schedule.
5.
Power Purchase Agreements
Understanding the importance of long-
term revenue assurance, we assist clients in
negotiating and finalizing power purchase
agreements (PPAs) with distribution
companies (DISCOMs) and other off-takers.
Our involvement in this process helps
create mutually beneficial partnerships that
maximize returns and support the continued
growth of renewable energy in India.

FROM THE FOUNDER'S DESK
Winds of
progress
सही समय पर, सही तरीके से,
सही चीज़ करना ही सफलता है
Success is doing the right thing,
in the right way, at the right time.
Dear Shareholders,
I hope this letter finds
you in good health.
As we reflect on the past year, I
am filled with pride and gratitude
for the support and dedication of
our employees, clients, and, most
importantly, you - our valued
shareholders. Your belief in our
collective vision for India's green
energy transition continues to
inspire us to reach new heights.
Winds of Change in
Renewable Energy
The renewable energy sector,
and within it wind energy,
is undergoing an era of
unprecedented growth and
transformation. Under the
National Electricity Plan for
2022-2032, India's installed wind
capacity is projected to reach 73
GW by 2026-27 and 122 GW by
2031-32. Achieving and sustaining
this momentum will require large-
scale investments, enhanced
grid infrastructure, streamlined
regulations, and continuous
technological innovation.
India's wind energy sector is
poised to play a pivotal role in the
nation's green energy transition,
supported by progressive
policy, regulatory framework,
and infrastructure initiatives. The
government's push to modernize
transmission infrastructure -
through the Green Energy Corridor
and HVDC networks - has been
instrumental in unlocking new
opportunities.
Following the government's
ambitious target of ~50 GW of
renewable energy and ~10 GW of
onshore wind bids per year (2023-
2027), last year witnessed a surge in
industry activity. By December 2024,
nearly 27.3 GW of projects were
awarded, either as standalone wind
or as components of hybrid projects.
The offshore wind sector is also
gaining momentum, with a 4 GW
tender in Tamil Nadu and a 500 MW
project off the coast of Gujarat -
initiatives that leverage India's vast
coastline and wind potential while
reducing reliance on conventional
power.
Growing Order Book
KP Energy continues to see the
effects of these tailwinds with robust
business momentum and strong
revenue visibility for the coming
years, as reflected in our significant
2.26+ GW business pipeline. This
includes projects across various
segments, catering to both CPP and
IPP customers in wind and wind-
solar hybrid domains.
Notable projects include a 308
MW project for Indian Oil NTPC
Green Energy Limited (INGEL),
multiple projects with Aditya Birla
Group entities, and an order book
exceeding 1 GW from our own group
company, KPI Green Energy. The
scale of these orders underscores
the exponential potential for scaling
our operations in the coming year.
Other marquee projects with NTPC
Renewable, Micro Life Sciences,
Ayana Renewable Power, and
Seven Renewable Power further
strengthen our multi-year revenue
visibility, enabling us to pursue
new growth opportunities with a
targeted and selective approach.
Leadership and
Innovation
Our strategic focus on turnkey
solutions and our comprehensive
presence across the wind energy
value chain have been pivotal in
maintaining our market-leading
position in Gujarat. We remain
Gujarat's premier BOP turnkey
solution provider for utility-scale
wind energy projects, benefiting
from essential infrastructure,
available land bank, robust power
evacuation connectivity, and deep
expertise in project execution.
KP Energy is also actively exploring
strategic initiatives, including the
development of 1-2 GW offshore
wind projects in Gujarat and
Tamil Nadu. While we maintain
our leadership in onshore project
execution, we are equally attuned
to the immense potential in the
offshore segment, in line with the
government's growing focus on
this domain.
We are also proud to be the first
Company to advance wind resource
deployment in South Gujarat. As
we continue to grow our flagship
EPCC projects business, we remain
committed to financial stability
and business model diversification,
ensuring resilience across business
cycles. Expanding our IPP portfolio
from the current 48.5 MW to
100+ MW by FY27 remains a core
strategic objective on this front.
Delivering on Our
Promise
FY25 has been another exceptional
year for KP Energy. We achieved a
significant increase in our top line,
with Revenue from Operations
reaching ₹939 Crores - a 99%
year-on-year growth from ₹473
Crores in FY24.
Profitability improved markedly,
with EBITDA doubling from ₹98
Crores in FY24 to ₹196 Crores in
FY25. Profit After Tax reached
a record high of ₹115 Crores,
representing a 98% rise over last
year's ₹58 Crores. Earnings Per
Share (EPS) surged from ₹8.8 in
FY24 to ₹17.3 in FY25.
This robust financial performance
was underpinned by strong
project execution and enhanced
operational efficiency. KP Energy
has now successfully energized
wind projects exceeding 1 GW in
its lifetime, spanning both CTU
and STU projects. Our ongoing
commitment to leveraging
advanced technology to streamline
operations and accelerate project
delivery has been instrumental
in achieving these results. At the
same time, we have also built a
robust balance sheet, empowering
us to pursue future growth
opportunities with confidence.
A Future Full of Promise
In conclusion, the future of KP
Energy is bright - underpinned
by a significant business pipeline,
robust project execution
capabilities, and our pole position
in the industry. The favourable
macro-economic environment
for renewables, combined with
our strategic initiatives and strong
financial performance, positions us
well to capitalize on the growing
opportunities in the renewable
energy sector.
We remain steadfast in our
commitment to delivering value
to our shareholders, clients, and all
stakeholders while contributing
to India's ambitious renewable
energy goals.
I extend my heartfelt appreciation
to our employees for their hard
work and dedication, to our clients
for their continued trust, and to
you, our shareholders, for your
support. Together, we will continue
to drive KP Energy towards a
sustainable and prosperous future.
With warm regards and gratitude,
DR. FARUK G. PATEL
Promoter |
Managing Director

WHOLE TIME DIRECTOR'S LETTER TO SHAREHOLDERS
Driving growth
and innovation
Dear Shareholders,
It is with great pleasure and
gratitude that I present to you the
Annual Report for KP Energy for the
Financial Year 2024-25. On behalf of
the Board of Directors and the entire
KP Energy team, I extend my sincere
appreciation for your continued
trust, which has been instrumental
in driving our company to new
heights.
Continuing Project
Momentum
FY25 has been a landmark year for
KP Energy, as we scaled greater
heights and reinforced our position
as a leader in the wind energy
sector.
Our project execution capabilities
have set new benchmarks, with
the successful delivery of large-
scale utility projects within record
timelines - an evidence to our
commitment for excellence and
innovation.
Among our key achievements,
we commissioned a marquee
252 MW project at our Sidhpur-II
site for Apraava Energy, involving
the commissioning of 120 Wind
Turbine Generators. Additionally, we
completed a 28.6 MW project at the
Vagra site for one of our esteemed
IPP clients. Progress continues at full
pace on several significant ongoing
projects, including a 308 MW project
for Indian Oil NTPC Green Energy,
major projects for Aditya Birla Group
entities, and some for our own
group company, KPI Green Energy.
Our current portfolio of projects in
hand totals over 2.26+ GW, spanning
a diversified pool of CPP and IPP
clients and covering both wind
and wind-solar hybrid projects.
This robust pipeline underscores
our dedication to expanding our
footprint and delivering exceptional
value to our clients.
Operational
Developments
I am pleased to share several
strategic operational developments
that have further strengthened our
foundation this year. Your Company
is actively enabling resource creation
for PE connectivity across both STU
and CTU networks. We have applied
for 100 MW ISTS connectivity, now in
the advanced approval stage, which
will facilitate interstate sales and
enhance our market reach.
As part of our forward-looking
strategy, we are deploying wind
mast installations to assess wind
potential for future wind and hybrid
projects totalling 1-2 GW across
Gujarat, Rajasthan, and Madhya
Pradesh. This initiative not only
provides better visibility for future
project locations but also serves
as a core value proposition for our
turnkey solutions to clients.
A significant technological
milestone was achieved with the
induction of the advanced 4X MW
platform. We recently placed an
order for Senvion India's 4.2 MW
M160 Wind Turbine Generators
(WTGs), which will be installed in our
upcoming hybrid power projects
in Bharuch district. This marks
one of the earliest commercial
deployments of Senvion's most
advanced turbine, optimized for
low wind conditions in India and
designed to maximize output and
durability. This technology will
ensure higher PLF achievability in
our upcoming projects.
Building on Capabilities
Our journey of growth is
underpinned by continuous
investment in capabilities and
people. By leveraging our expertise
across STU and CTU networks,
diversifying power evacuation
pathways, and broadening
our customer base, we are
positioning ourselves well for future
opportunities.
We have made significant
investments in technology
initiatives, including a state-of-the-
art Network Operation Centre for
real-time monitoring, diagnostics,
and control of our energy
generation assets. Our adoption of
LIDAR technology for wind resource
assessment further enhances our
project evaluation and monitoring
capabilities, ensuring operational
continuity and superior project
performance.
Equally important is our focus
on strengthening management
bandwidth and leadership
development. I am delighted to
announce the appointment of Dr.
Alok Das as Group CEO. Dr. Das, an
IIT-Kanpur alumnus with over 30
years of renewable energy expertise,
brings invaluable experience from
leadership roles at Suzlon and
Reliance. I am pleased to welcome
him to the KP Group family.
To nurture our next generation of
leaders, we have partnered with
IIM Ahmedabad for a Leadership
Excellence Program, fostering
talent and preparing our team for
future challenges. Our emphasis
on people development and a
positive work culture has once
again earned us the prestigious
'Great Place to Work' certification.
Outlook
Looking ahead, the outlook for KP
Energy remains very promising,
supported by our significant 2.26+
GW business pipeline in both
standalone wind and wind-solar
hybrid projects. Our ongoing
discussions with renowned IPP
and CPP customers for large-
scale projects in Gujarat and
beyond reflect our commitment
to expanding our footprint and
industry leadership.
We are also focused on expanding
our own IPP portfolio, targeting 100
MW of energy-generating assets
under the IPP segment within the
next two years. The growth in our
own energy generation assets has
started to create a stable annuity
income, further strengthening our
financial foundation.
As India accelerates its transition
to renewable energy, I am
confident that KP Energy is poised
to play a pivotal role in shaping
a sustainable energy future for
the nation. Our industry-leading
capabilities, robust pipeline, and
dedicated team position us for
continued success and value
creation for all stakeholders.
Thank you once again for your
unwavering trust and support.
Warm regards,
AFFAN FARUK PATEL
Whole-Time Director

GROUP CEO LETTER TO SHAREHOLDERS
From vision
to velocity
Dear Shareholders,
It gives me great pride and
satisfaction to address you at
the close of what has been a
truly transformative year for our
company. The past twelve months
have been marked by tremendous
growth, strategic advancements,
and bold steps that have positioned
us at the forefront of India's
renewable energy landscape.
Together, with your continued
support and confidence, we have
not only expanded our operational
footprint but also strengthened our
long-term foundations to create
enduring value for all stakeholders.
In-House Wind Resource
Assessment Team
One of the cornerstones of our
growth strategy this year has
been the establishment of a
fully integrated, in-house Wind
Resource Assessment (WRA) team.
Traditionally, companies have relied
on external consultants for site
assessment and wind studies.
While effective, this approach
often comes with limitations in
terms of speed, customization,
and cost efficiency. By creating
our own dedicated WRA team, we
have gained a significant strategic
advantage.
Our in-house experts are now
able to conduct high-precision
assessments of wind potential
at prospective sites, leveraging
state-of-the-art measurement
instruments and advanced
modelling tools. This not only
shortens the development cycle but
also allows us to optimize project
design, ensuring maximum energy
yield and efficiency. Beyond the
immediate financial benefits, this
initiative strengthens our intellectual
capital, giving us a sharper edge
in competitive bidding scenarios
and enabling us to enter into new
geographies with confidence.
The results have already begun
to show. With our in-house
assessments, we have successfully
identified high-quality sites that
are now progressing rapidly toward
development. Moreover, the cost
savings generated are being
reinvested into innovation and
capacity expansion. This is a clear
example of how building internal
capabilities can yield multiplier
effects on growth and profitability.
Adoption of Next-
Generation Wind Turbine
Generators (WTGs)
Another decisive factor in our
growth this year has been the
adoption of next-generation Wind
Turbine Generators in the 4.X
MW class and beyond. The Indian
wind sector has historically been
dominated by smaller turbine
models up to 3 MW platform.
While effective, these turbines are
gradually being eclipsed by the
superior efficiency, higher yield
factors, and improved economics of
the larger models. We have already
partnered with Senvion India for
their upcoming 4.2MW WTG, being
the first developers to use their
flagship product.
Our move to deploy 4.X WTGs and
above puts us at the cutting edge of
industry trends. These machines are
capable of delivering significantly
higher output per turbine, enabling
us to generate more energy with
fewer turbines and a smaller land
footprint. This translates directly
into lower Levelized Cost of Energy
(LCoE), making our projects not
only more profitable but also more
competitive in tariff-based auctions.
Furthermore, the adoption of
larger turbines aligns with our
sustainability vision. With higher
yields and optimized layouts, we
are able to minimize land use,
reduce balance-of-plant costs,
and deliver projects that are both
environmentally and economically
superior. Several of our new projects
under execution already feature
these state-of-the-art WTGs,
and the results have been very
encouraging.
The Road Ahead
As we look forward, the trajectory
of our growth is clear. Our strategy
rests on three pillars: building
unmatched internal capabilities,
embracing technological
advancements, and venturing into
new frontiers of renewable energy.
Each of these pillars reinforces the
other, creating a cycle of innovation,
efficiency, and expansion.
The coming year will see us
consolidating our onshore wind
portfolio, accelerating progress on
offshore feasibility, and scaling up
the deployment of 4.X WTGs. At the
same time, we remain committed
to operational excellence, financial
prudence, and shareholder value
creation. Our ambition is not only
to grow but to grow responsibly
delivering clean energy solutions
that power India's sustainable
future while rewarding the trust
and confidence of our investors.
Gratitude and
Acknowledgement
None of these achievements
would have been possible without
the unwavering dedication of
our employees, the trust of our
partners, and most importantly,
the confidence of you-our
shareholders. Your continued
support enables us to take bold
steps, innovate fearlessly, and
pursue opportunities that define
the future of renewable energy in
India and beyond.
On behalf of the entire leadership
team, I extend my deepest
gratitude. Together, we are
shaping an organization that not
only creates financial value but
also contributes meaningfully to
society and the environment.
With conviction in our vision and
confidence in our strategy, I am
excited about the journey ahead.
The past year has set a strong
foundation, and the years to come
hold even greater promise.
Warm regards,
DR. ALOK DAS
Group CEO

CORPORATE GOVERNANCE FRAMEWORK
Upholding transparency
and accountability
KP Energy is resolute in its commitment to maintaining the highest
standards of corporate governance, recognizing that robust governance
is the cornerstone of sustainable growth and long-term value creation
for all stakeholders. The Company's approach is rooted in transparency,
accountability, and ethical conduct, which are deeply embedded in its
organizational culture and operational framework.
Commitment to
Transparency and
Accountability
KP Energy fosters open and
transparent communication
with all stakeholders, ensuring
timely and accurate disclosure
of information. The Company's
governance framework is
designed to facilitate effective
decision-making, prudent risk
management, and the prompt
dissemination of material
information, empowering
stakeholders to make informed
decisions and engage
meaningfully with the business.
Ethical Conduct and
Governance Philosophy
Ethical conduct is integral to KP
Energy's governance philosophy.
The Company has implemented a
comprehensive Code of Conduct
for its Board members and
senior management, which sets
out the expected standards of
integrity and professionalism.
Compliance with this Code is
affirmed annually, reinforcing the
Company's dedication to ethical
business practices and regulatory
compliance.
KP Energy's governance
philosophy extends beyond
regulatory compliance,
emphasizing the importance of
integrity, fairness, and efficiency
in all interactions. The Company
believes that sound governance
practices are essential for
achieving its long-term objectives
and enhancing stakeholder trust
and value.
Governance Framework
and Policies
The Company's governance
framework encompasses a wide
array of systems and practices,
including:
. Board Diversity Policy: We
KP Energy's Board Diversity
Policy ensures a balanced
mix of Executive, Non-
Executive Non-Independent,
and Independent Directors
from diverse backgrounds,
fostering a broad spectrum
of perspectives and expertise.
The policy emphasizes merit-
based appointments while
promoting inclusivity and
equal opportunity, in line with
SEBI Listing Regulations.
. Internal Controls: The
Company has established
robust internal financial control
systems, regularly reviewed
to ensure adequacy and
effectiveness. These controls
safeguard assets, ensure
accurate financial reporting,
and facilitate compliance with
applicable laws and policies.
. Internal Controls: The Company
has established robust internal
financial control systems,
regularly reviewed to ensure
adequacy and effectiveness.
These controls safeguard assets,
ensure accurate financial
reporting, and facilitate
compliance with applicable laws
and policies.
. Vigil Mechanism and Whistle
Blower Policy: KP Energy has
instituted a Whistle Blower Policy
and vigil mechanism, providing
directors and employees with
a secure channel to report
genuine concerns about
unethical or improper activities,
free from fear of retaliation.
Direct access to the Chairman of
the Audit Committee is available
in exceptional cases, ensuring
transparency and accountability
at all levels.
. Board Committees: The Board
is supported by specialized
committees such as the Audit
Committee, Nomination and
Remuneration Committee,
Stakeholders Relationship
Committee, Risk Management
Committee, and Corporate
Social Responsibility Committee
which oversee critical aspects
of governance and risk
management.
Board of Directors
Independence of
Composition
The Board of Directors at KP
Energy comprises a balanced mix
of Executive, Non-Executive, and
Independent Directors, ensuring
independent judgment and
diversity of thought. As of March 31,
2025, the Board consists of ten
(10) members, including three
(3) Executive Directors, three (3)
Non-Executive Non-Independent
Directors, and four (4) Independent
Directors. This composition ensures
independence of the Board.

Board Committees
To uphold the highest standards of governance, the Board has established several specialized committees, each
entrusted with distinct roles and responsibilities. These committees ensure focused oversight and facilitate
informed decision-making across key areas:
Audit Committee
The Audit Committee is responsible
for supervising the financial
reporting process, internal control
mechanisms, and audit activities.
Comprising one Executive
Director and two Independent
Directors, the committee ensures
an independent and objective
review of the Company's financial
statements. By diligently
monitoring the integrity, accuracy,
and transparency of financial
disclosures, the Audit Committee
plays a crucial role in maintaining
the credibility and compliance of
the Company's financial reporting.
Nomination and
Remuneration
Committee
Charged with identifying and
recommending suitable candidates
for Board appointments, this
committee also evaluates the
performance of Directors and
determines the remuneration for
Directors and senior management.
The committee carefully assesses
the skills, knowledge, and
experience of potential appointees,
ensuring the Board possesses the
right blend of expertise to drive
effective governance.
Stakeholders'
Relationship Committee
This committee is dedicated to
addressing the concerns and
grievances of shareholders and
other stakeholders. It acts as a
central platform for resolving
issues related to share transfers,
non-receipt of annual reports,
dividends, and other matters,
thereby fostering transparent
and responsive communication
with all stakeholders.
Risk Management
Committee
The Risk Management Committee
oversees the company's risk
framework, covering financial,
operational, sectoral, ESG, cyber
security, and other key risks. It
ensures strong internal controls,
mitigation measures, and
business continuity planning;
reviews the Risk Policy at least
every two years; monitors
system effectiveness; handles
matters related to the Chief
Risk Officer; coordinates with
other Board committees;
and reports its activities and
recommendations to the Board.
Corporate Social
Responsibility (CSR)
Committee
The CSR Committee is tasked
with formulating and overseeing
the implementation of the
Company's CSR policy. It ensures
that KP Energy's initiatives
contribute meaningfully to
societal and environmental
well-being. The committee is
responsible for recommending
and monitoring the allocation
of resources for all CSR activities,
reaffirming the Company's
commitment to responsible and
sustainable business practices.
Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) Initiatives
KP Energy, being in the renewable energy sector, inherently contributes to environmental sustainability
through its core business. Beyond this, the Company has implemented various ESG initiatives:

gas emissions

reduction goals

Dr. Faruk G. Patel
Managing Director

the logistics sector, transitioned through the construction
and infrastructure industries, and ultimately ventured into

from American East Coast University, New York, USA.
Dr. Patel is the driving force that has been instrumental
in the overall success of KP Group since 1994.

operational efficiency. Under his leadership, the Company
has expanded its presence in the renewable energy
sector through innovative strategies focused on customer
engagement, performance improvement, and sustainable
profitability.

Whole Time Director

Promoting Education

for underprivileged communities
through comprehensive medical

donation drives, general medical
support, eye operations, dental
services, and clinic facilities. We've
consistently provided medical
assistance across Gujarat, ensuring
healthcare reaches those who need
it most while supporting preventive
healthcare measures.

The KP Human Development
Foundation has constructed 3
Amrit Sarovar ponds in Dwarka
and Siddhpur, providing essential
drinking water to nearby villages.

recognition from the District

developed drinking water ponds

employment and skill development.

enhanced prospects. This approach
fosters economic independence,
strengthening bonds between KP
Energy and served communities.
We take pride in offering
professional growth opportunities in
rural areas, particularly through rural
development projects in Bharuch
and sports training initiatives that
empower rural talent in Surat.

society's most vulnerable members.

to orphans through basic amenities
and improved living conditions in

programs uplift underprivileged
women, promoting gender equality.
We also honor our nation's heroes by
supporting armed forces veterans,
war widows, and their dependents
in Surat, recognizing their invaluable
service.

also include facilitating renewable
energy adoption in Surat, such as
15KW rooftop solar systems, and
supporting wildlife conservation
and animal welfare programs that
protect native species at various
locations.
Elder Care Initiative
The KP Human Development
Foundation is building "Prabhu
nu Ghar," an old-age home in
Uchediya village, Jhagadia taluka,
Bharuch. This facility will house 200
elderly individuals along the scenic
Narmada River banks. Our senior
citizen support initiatives in Bharuch
include comprehensive civil work,
construction services, and design
assistance for day care centers
and old-age facilities. The project,
backed by the Disable Welfare Trust
of India under Dr. Kanubhai Tailor's
leadership, aligns with our broader
social welfare mission.

Management
Discussion and
Analysis
Economic Overview
Indian economy
India's economy in 2025 stands out
for its resilience and dynamism
amid a challenging global
environment, according to the latest
publications from the IMF and the
World Bank. According to the IMF,
India's GDP growth is projected
to be at 6.2% for 2025 and 6.3% for
2026, maintaining its status as the
fastest-growing major economy
in the world. This forecast reflects
the impact of global trade tensions
and policy uncertainties that have
affected economies worldwide.
The World Bank, in its January
2025 update, had projected India's
growth at 6.7% for the fiscal year
2025-26, but revised this figure to
6.3% in April 2025, citing a slowdown
in public investment and persistent
global economic weakness.
Despite these downward revisions,
India's growth remains robust,
especially when compared to
other major economies. The IMF
highlights that India's growth rate
is more than double the global
average forecast of 2.8% for 2025.
This impressive performance is
underpinned by several key drivers.
Private consumption, particularly
in rural areas, has shown notable
recovery, supported by improved
agricultural output and government
welfare initiatives. The services
sector continues to be a major
engine of growth, with strong
performances in information
technology, financial services,
and tourism. Government-led
infrastructure development,
especially in transportation and
energy, has provided additional
impetus, although public capital
expenditure has not met earlier
targets.
Private investment is expected to
gain momentum in the coming
months, bolstered by the Reserve
Bank of India's accommodative
monetary policy and ongoing
regulatory reforms aimed at
improving the business climate.
However, the manufacturing sector's
growth has been relatively subdued,
constrained by weak export demand
and lasting supply chain disruptions.
The external sector faces additional
challenges, as global trade policy
shifts and economic slowdowns in
key markets have dampened export
prospects. As a result, the current
account deficit is projected to widen
moderately, although it remains
manageable within the broader
context of India's macroeconomic
stability.
India's economic ascent is also
reflected in its global rankings. In
2025, India is set to become the
world's fourth-largest economy by
nominal GDP, overtaking Japan with
an estimated GDP of $4.19 trillion.
Looking ahead, projections indicate
that India will surpass Germany by
2028, securing the position of the
third-largest economy globally.
This rapid rise is seen by the
IMF as part of a broader shift in
global economic power, with
India outpacing most other major
economies in both growth rate and
absolute size.
Nevertheless, some risks remain.
Ongoing global trade disputes,
could weigh on investor sentiment
and economic performance. The
IMF warns of potential spill overs
from rising trade tariffs and volatility
in global financial markets, which
could affect capital flows and
exchange rates. Both institutions
stress the importance of continued
reforms in infrastructure, financial
inclusion, and the regulatory
environment to sustain growth and
enhance resilience.
In summary, India's strong
economic trajectory, driven by
resilient domestic demand,
a vibrant services sector, and
strategic policy initiatives. While
challenges persist-especially in
manufacturing and exports - India's
fundamentals remain sound, and
its role as a key anchor of global
growth is increasingly recognized.
The confidence expressed by these
leading international institutions
underscores India's evolving
position as a major force in the
global economy, poised for further
advancement in the years ahead.
TTT
Industry overview
Indian renewable energy
landscape
According to the International
Energy Agency's World Energy
Outlook, the global supply of
renewable energy is projected
to reach 4,550 GW by 2040, and
India is set to play a significant role
in this expansion. Over the past
decade, India's installed renewable
power generation capacity has
grown at a compound annual
growth rate (CAGR) of 15.29%
between FY16 and FY25, reflecting
one of the fastest adoption rates
among major economies. As of
January 2025, India's total installed
renewable energy capacity reached
an impressive 165.2 GW, a figure
that underscores the country's
rapid progress in this sector. This
capacity now accounts for 45.5% of
the nation's total installed power
capacity, a remarkable shift from
the fossil fuel-dominated energy
mix of previous decades.
The Government of India's vision for
the future is even more ambitious.
India aims to achieve 450 GW
of installed renewable energy
capacity by 2030, with over 60% of
this - roughly 280 GW - expected to
come from solar power alone. This
target not only positions India as
a global leader in solar energy but
also opens up massive investment
opportunities, estimated at US$ 221
billion by 2030. The Ministry of New
and Renewable Energy has set an
even higher goal of 500 GW of non-
fossil-based electricity generation
capacity by 2030, in line with the
Prime Minister's announcement
at COP26. In 2023, the country
added 13.5 GW of renewable
energy capacity, corresponding to
an investment of around ₹74,000
Crores (US$ 8.90 billion), signalling
the scale and pace of ongoing
efforts.
The momentum is not limited to
central government initiatives. States
like Karnataka have set their own
ambitious targets, aiming to add
approximately 38 GW of renewable
energy capacity by FY35, with nearly
half of that expected by FY30. Such
regional efforts complement the
national agenda, ensuring that the
transition to clean energy is both
broad-based and inclusive.

MANAGEMENT DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS (CONTD.)
The impact of these investments
and policies is evident in the
continuous rise in power generation
from renewable sources. In
December 2024, power generation
from renewables (excluding hydro)
stood at 19.77 billion units (BU), up
from 16.76 BU in December 2023.
For the period between April and
December 2024, renewable energy
sources generated 189.48 BU, up 10%
YOY, marking a significant increase
from 172.48 BU in the same period
the previous year. These figures
highlight not only the growing
capacity but also the increasing
reliability and contribution of
renewables to India's energy grid.
India's achievements in wind
energy are equally noteworthy. The
country now ranks fourth globally in
terms of total installed wind power
capacity, reflecting its long-standing
commitment to harnessing this
resource. The National Electricity
Plan (Transmission) further supports
the renewable sector by aiming to
boost interregional transmission
capacity to 168 GW by 2031-32.
This involves adding 191,474 circuit
kilometres of transmission lines
and 1,274 GVA of transformation
capacity, including 33.25 GW of
high-voltage direct current (HVDC)
links, cross-border interconnections,
and increased private sector
participation to enhance grid
efficiency and reliability.
Solar energy, in particular, has
witnessed a phenomenal rise. India
added 16.4 GW of solar capacity
during January-September 2024,
marking a 167% YOY increase. By
September 2024, India's cumulative
installed solar capacity stood at
89.1 GW, with utility-scale projects
making up over 86% and rooftop
solar accounting for nearly 14%.
Solar power now constitutes
approximately 20% of India's total
installed power capacity and more
than 44% of its renewable energy
capacity, underscoring its central
role in the country's clean energy
strategy.
Realizing these ambitious targets,
however, requires substantial
investment. Moody's estimates
that India will need between US$
190 billion and US$ 215 billion over
the next seven years to achieve the
500 GW renewable energy capacity
target by 2030. Additionally, another
US$ 150 billion to US$ 170 billion
will be necessary for electricity
transmission, distribution, and
energy storage infrastructure.
These figures highlight the scale of
financial mobilization required and
the critical role of both public and
private sector investment in driving
the transition.
Despite the impressive progress,
challenges remain. Grid integration
of variable renewable sources, land
acquisition for large-scale projects,
and the need for technological
advancements in storage and
transmission are persistent hurdles.
The government and industry
stakeholders are addressing these
issues through policy reforms,
innovative financing mechanisms,
and the adoption of advanced
technologies such as smart grids
and battery storage. The focus
on enhancing interregional
transmission capacity, encouraging
private sector participation, and
fostering cross-border energy
cooperation are strategic steps
toward overcoming these obstacles.
In conclusion, India's renewable
energy landscape in 2025 is
characterized by rapid growth,
ambitious targets, and a strong
policy framework. With nearly half
of its power capacity now coming
from non-fossil sources and solar
energy at the forefront, India is
well on its way to achieving its
2030 goals. The sector's growth not
only contributes to energy security
and environmental sustainability
but also offers vast economic
opportunities, positioning India as
a global leader in the clean energy
transition. The coming years will
be crucial as the country seeks to
scale up investments, enhance
grid infrastructure, and integrate
cutting-edge technologies to
realize its vision of a sustainable and
prosperous energy future.
Indian wind energy
sector
Strategic Vision and
National Targets
India's wind energy sector is a
cornerstone of the country's
ambitious clean energy transition.
The government has launched
several strategic initiatives, including
a target of 500 GW of renewable
energy capacity by 2030 (with 140
GW from wind), a net zero goal
by 2070, and the 'National Green
Hydrogen Mission'. These initiatives
are designed to drive both energy
security and sustainable growth.
India's wind energy sector is
witnessing strong growth, with 3.4
GW of new capacity added in 2024
- the highest annual installation
level since 2017 - bringing total
capacity to 48.16 GW. India ranks 4th
globally in total wind installations,
with 48.16 GW of onshore wind
capacity, making it the 2nd largest
wind market in the Asia Pacific
region after China. This growth is the
result of decades of policy support,
technological advancements, and
a robust domestic manufacturing
ecosystem.
Growth Trajectory and
Capacity Outlook
India's wind sector has experienced
a resurgence, with over 4 GW of
new wind power capacity installed
in 2024-25, a significant increase
over previous years. The Global Wind
Energy Council (GWEC) projects
a continued recovery, revising its
onshore wind outlook for 2024-
2028 to 22.8 GW. According to the
National Electricity Plan, installed
wind capacity is estimated to reach
around 73 GW by 2026-27 and
122 GW by 2031-32. Despite this
momentum, a sizable gap remains
between projected growth and the
government's 140 GW wind target
for 2030.
Policy and Regulatory
Framework
The central government, through
the Ministry of New and Renewable
Energy (MNRE), has outlined an
ambitious procurement trajectory,
mandating annual bids of 50 GW for
solar, wind, and hybrid projects from
FY24 to FY28.
Following the government's
announcement that it was targeting
50 GW of renewable energy and 10
GW of onshore wind bids per year
between 2023 and 2027 through
single-stage/e-reverse auction
bidding, last year saw a surge in
activity. By the end of December
2024, nearly 27.3 GW of projects had
been awarded, either as standalone
wind or as wind components of
hybrid projects. Gujarat, Tamil
Nadu, Maharashtra, Rajasthan and
Karnataka led the way for India's
2024 wind energy auctions.

. Plans to upgrade the

. Policy measures in support of

and green electricity purchase

. Timely disbursal of payments by

Waivers on Inter-State Transmission

commissioned before June 2025 have
also reduced costs and encouraged
interstate power sales. The allowance
of 100% Foreign Direct Investment
(FDI) under the automatic route
has attracted international capital
and expertise, fostering technology
transfer and innovation.

Manufacturing and Cost
Competitiveness
India's wind energy industry is
supported by a strong domestic
manufacturing sector, with an
annual production capacity of
around 18 GW for wind turbine
components. This has reduced
import dependence and fostered a
vibrant ecosystem of suppliers and
skilled labour. Wind power remains
cost-competitive, with recent
auction tariffs ranging from ₹2.43
to ₹3.17 per kWh, though supply
chain and financing challenges
have recently increased prices.
India is strategically positioned for
wind manufacturing expansion,
benefiting from the "China +1"
approach adopted by global
supply chain factors. Domestic
manufacturing is sufficient to meet
onshore wind demand through
2030, with potential for export
growth if capacity is scaled up.
Offshore Wind: Progress and
Prospects
India is making significant strides
in offshore wind. The first offshore
wind seabed tender has been
finalized, with a revised strategy
paper outlining 3 models to
award 37 GW of capacity by 2030.
Offshore wind lease rules have been
released, and in February 2024,
SECI announced a 4 GW offshore
wind seabed leasing in Tamil Nadu.
Viability gap funding (VGF) has
been approved for an initial 1 GW of
offshore wind, along with an ISTS
waiver up to 2032.
To realize offshore wind potential,
India must address market barriers
such as port and grid infrastructure
readiness, vessel availability, supply
chain strategy, offtake assurance,
streamlined permitting, community
partnership, and skilled workforce
development. The successful award
of offshore wind tenders is expected
to attract investments in domestic
offshore wind manufacturing, with
incentives from central and state
governments playing a crucial role.
Outlook
India's wind energy sector is poised
for robust growth, underpinned
by a clear policy roadmap, strong
manufacturing base, and increasing
private sector participation.
The sector is central to India's
decarbonization strategy, supporting
industrial decarbonization and
the sustainability commitments
of Indian and multinational
corporations. With continued policy
support and market reforms, wind
energy will play a pivotal role in
India's clean energy transition,
economic development, and climate
resilience in the years ahead.

MANAGEMENT DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS (CONTD.)
Hybrid Renewable
Energy Projects
The wind-solar hybrid energy space
in India has rapidly emerged as a
strategic solution to the challenges
of renewable energy integration,
grid stability, and cost optimization.
As of September 2024, India's
operational wind-solar hybrid
project capacity stood at ~7.7 GW,
with a robust project pipeline of
nearly ~30 GW, underscoring the
sector's accelerating momentum
and growing investor confidence, as
per Mercom India. Hybrid systems,
which combine wind turbines and
solar photovoltaic (PV) panels - often
supplemented by Battery Energy
Storage Systems (BESS) - leverage
the complementary generation
profiles of wind and solar resources.
Solar power typically peaks during
the day, while wind generation
often rises at night or during
monsoon months, resulting in a
more stable and predictable overall
power output. This synergy not
only reduces intermittency but also
allows for higher capacity utilization
factors (CUF), making hybrid plants
more efficient and reliable than
standalone wind or solar projects.
The Indian government has
recognized the strategic value of
hybridization and has introduced
supportive policies and regulatory
frameworks to accelerate
deployment. The Ministry of New
and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
has facilitated large-scale hybrid
tenders, with the Solar Energy
Corporation of India (SECI) playing
a pivotal role in auctioning hybrid
capacities. Recent auctions have
demonstrated the economic
viability of these projects: for
instance, a 1,200 MW wind-solar
hybrid tender in 2024 saw tariffs in
the range of ₹3.43-3.46 per kWh,
with a minimum CUF requirement
of 30%. In comparison, recent
standalone wind and solar auctions
yielded tariffs of ₹3.60-3.70 per kWh
(wind, CUF 22%) and ₹2.48 per kWh
(solar, CUF 17%), respectively. The
higher CUF and competitive tariffs
of hybrid projects highlight their
ability to optimize transmission
infrastructure and reduce overall
capital costs, making them
attractive to both developers and
distribution companies, as per
Ember.
Major projects are now underway
to harness the full potential of
hybrid systems. Notably, India
has announced a 13 GW hybrid
renewable energy park in Ladakh,
integrating solar, wind, and
battery storage across the Pang,
Debring, and Kharnak regions. This
ambitious initiative not only aims
to maximize renewable generation
in a high-potential area but also
includes the development of a
dedicated interstate transmission
system under the Green Energy
Corridor (GEC-II) program to
evacuate power to demand centers
in northern India. Such projects
exemplify the scale and complexity
of India's hybrid ambitions, which
are increasingly seen as essential for
meeting national renewable energy
targets and ensuring grid reliability.
The advantages of wind-solar
hybrid systems extend beyond grid
stability and cost savings.
By diversifying generation sources,
hybrid plants enhance energy
security, reduce reliance on fossil
fuels, and offer a pathway to
energy independence for remote
or underserved regions. Advanced
control systems, such as SCADA, are
integral to these projects, enabling
real-time monitoring, optimization,
and seamless integration with
smart grids. Battery storage further
enhances the value proposition by
enabling time-shifting of excess
generation and providing ancillary
services to the grid.
Despite these benefits, the sector
faces challenges related to land
acquisition, timely grid connectivity,
and the need for streamlined
auction and approval processes.
Addressing these issues is critical
to unlocking the full potential
of hybrids and meeting the
government's ambitious renewable
energy targets. As India's energy mix
evolves, wind-solar hybrid systems
are poised to play a central role
in delivering reliable, affordable,
and sustainable power, driving the
nation closer to its decarbonization
goals and reinforcing its leadership
in the global renewable energy
transition.
Company overview
KP Energy Limited is a prominent
leader in the Indian wind energy
sector, distinguished by its
advanced and comprehensive
Balance of Plant (BOP) solutions
that drive the sustainable
development of large utility-
scale wind farms. Headquartered
in Gujarat, India, the Company
manages the complete lifecycle of
wind and wind-solar hybrid energy
projects - from conceptualization
and site identification to
construction, commissioning,
and ongoing operations and
maintenance through its post-
commissioning lifecycle. KP
Energy's comprehensive service
portfolio includes site preparation,
construction, erection, power
evacuation, and O&M, ensuring
seamless project execution
and delivery. The Company has
energised a cumulative installed
capacity of 1+ GW, with an additional
2.26+ GW in the pipeline.
With a strong commitment to
sustainability and innovation,
KP Energy integrates all critical
activities required for utility-scale
wind farm development, offering
turnkey solutions that streamline
project delivery. This holistic
approach positions the Company
as a key contributor to India's
renewable energy transition,
supporting a greener future
through reliable and efficient
renewable power infrastructure.
Business Model
KP Energy structures its operations around 3 core segments, each integral
to its leadership in India's wind energy market:

and Maintenance

KP Energy's O&M division focuses
on the effective management and
maintenance of wind energy BOP
infrastructure, ensuring long-term
reliability and optimal performance
of wind farms.
As an IPP, KP Energy develops,
owns, and operates wind and solar
assets, directly contributing to

and supporting the nation's shift
toward sustainable power sources.
